title: ByteWolf Cognitive Hook Proposal v0.1
description: A typed candidate emitted by a background LLM job. A proposal is a suggestion, never a committed fact: it flows proposal -> schema validation -> policy/admission -> canonical store, and deterministic admission code decides what, if anything, is kept. Generalises the post-turn memory hook's memory_delta. See docs/workstreams/cognitive-hooks.md.

contract_version.description: Pins the meaning of every field below. A consumer that does not know this version rejects the proposal rather than guess.

proposal_id.description: Unique id for this proposal, for correlation with the admission decision and audit.

kind.description: Which family this proposal belongs to. v0.1 admits only memory_delta; a new kind is a new contract version, not a silent extension.

source.description: Provenance for audit: which background job produced it, with which model and prompt version, over which inputs.

job.description: The background job that emitted the proposal, e.g. 'post_turn_memory'.

input_refs.description: Opaque references to the inputs the job saw (e.g. turn ids or content hashes), never the raw inputs.

created_at.description: RFC 3339, UTC, with an explicit offset. When the proposal was produced.

memory_delta.description: A set of durable-memory operations. Deliberately narrow in v0.1: only stable user facts, never mission commands or transient status.

operations.description: At least one operation; an empty delta is no proposal at all.

op.description: 'upsert' records or supersedes a fact; 'forget' removes one. Forgets are applied before upserts by the admission code.

category.description: The narrow set of durable categories v0.1 admits. Anything else is rejected, not stored.

value.description: The fact to record or forget. Admission code caps its length and rejects sensitive data (credentials, tokens, e-mail, phone, precise address).
